seoTitle: How to Record a Vehicle Lease in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Learn how to record a vehicle lease in QuickBooks Online, from setting up accounts to entering monthly payments, so your books stay accurate.
intro: Leasing a vehicle for your business comes with its own bookkeeping steps, different from recording a purchase. This guide covers how to set up the right accounts and enter lease payments correctly in QuickBooks Online.

steps.heading: Set up a lease liability or expense account
steps.body: Create the appropriate account to track the lease, depending on how your accountant wants it categorized.

steps.heading: Enter the lease agreement details
steps.body: Record the key terms of the lease, such as the monthly payment amount and lease term, for your own reference.

steps.heading: Record monthly lease payments
steps.body: Enter each payment as it's made, assigning it to the correct lease expense account.

steps.heading: Reconcile against your bank feed
steps.body: Match the recorded lease payments to your bank transactions to keep everything reconciled.

faq.question: Is a vehicle lease recorded differently than a loan?
faq.answer: Yes, a lease is typically recorded as an ongoing expense rather than as a liability tied to an owned asset, though the exact treatment can depend on the lease type.

faq.question: Can I deduct lease payments as a business expense?
faq.answer: Lease payments for a business vehicle are often deductible, but confirm the details with your accountant for your situation.

faq.question: Do I need to record the full lease value upfront?
faq.answer: No, you generally record each payment as it occurs rather than entering the entire lease value at once.

faq.question: What if the lease includes a down payment?
faq.answer: A down payment should be recorded as its own transaction, often allocated separately from the recurring monthly payments.
